Method-provenance block
Property: Atom-resolved Fukui functions (f+, f–, f0) and dual descriptor of ferrocene; most electrophilic/nucleophilic site and magnitude (e) reported here as headline number = f+ on Fe.
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding)
Geometry provenance: user-supplied /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/ferrocene.xyz; single-point charge analysis ‑ not re-optimized at this level.
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge / multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet parent; N–1 and N+1 states were doublets as generated by the skill).
Software: xtb via chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (engine_subcommand: fukui).
Convergence & caveats: run status ok; integrity.trustworthy = true. Warnings reproduced verbatim below. SCF cycles for N, N±1 states converged (sum of f+ and f– each 1.0000).

Selected results (e units)

• Most nucleophilic site (largest f–): atom 11 (Fe) f– = 0.25938191 e
